subject: Verification & Quality spine — test generation (test-at-birth, AC-driven), coverage maintenance, tiered pre-user quality gate, layered verification logging; cross-cutting across the v4 lifecycle
title: Verification & Quality Spine (v4)
situation: v4 has many verification pieces scattered with no unifying model: Pattern Gate (runtime verifier), bolt/check certification, verification_test on lugs (AC12), test_results table (storage layer), test-manifest, scouts, advisor certification, tiered verification (mechanical/attested/human), the two-pass count anti-pattern, and 'agent-tests-first' for user-facing work. Four questions are unanswered as a system: (1) how are tests generated, (2) how are suites + coverage maintained, (3) what quality bar is verified before the user sees anything, (4) is logging reviewable. This spec unifies them into one cross-cutting spine that threads through every lifecycle stage.
why: Without a unified spine, verification is ad hoc: tests get skipped at lug creation, coverage decays invisibly, the user is shown work that is 'well-formed on disk' but never actually run, and failures vanish silently. Each gap is a known WAI failure mode (skipping verification, mechanical-only on counts, 'probably saved'). The spine makes verification a hard, observable, maintained property: AC-traceable tests at birth, computed coverage owned by a QA advisor, a tiered pre-user gate, and a layered audit trail. Quality becomes verified, not asserted.
audience: lug-reviewer subagent — enforces test-at-birth gate (no draft→open without a runnable verification_test)
Pattern Gate subagent — post-gate enforces the pre-user quality bar per tier
QA advisor (spoke: Jordy/qa-engineer) — owns suite health, coverage computation, stale-test detection
Hub qa-guardian — aggregates fleet test health; Trainer reads post-adoption test deltas
Historian — mines test_results + gate-log for quality trends
every authoring agent — generates AC-traceable tests when creating a lug

q1_test_generation.principle: Test-at-birth, AC-driven. Tests are authored WHEN the lug is created, not after.
q1_test_generation.ac_traceability: Every acceptance criterion maps to >=1 test. The lug's verify[] steps are the test spec; they are materialized into runnable tests. A lug-reviewer gate FAILS draft->open if any AC has no test.

tiers.mechanical: a runnable assertion (script/CLI exit code, JSON schema check, file/state assertion). PREFERRED — deterministic, no hallucination. Script-backed wherever possible.
tiers.attested: a named AI verifier (e.g. pattern-gate, lug-reviewer) reads observable state and signs the result. Used when no deterministic assertion is feasible.
tiers.human: Mario signs. Reserved for taste/judgment calls the harness cannot assert.
tiers.attested_experiential: for user-facing semantic/experiential checks (does the page render? does the flow work?) — a Read+Bash gate structurally cannot do this (external review A: 'semantic/experiential testing void'). Route to a multimodal subagent in a SANDBOXED browser runtime (Playwright/headless) that logs structural visual/DOM assertions. Runs in the execution sandbox (spec-execution-sandbox-v1), not against live state.

generation_sources: lug authoring: agent writes verify[] -> materialized test (script-backed where possible)
scouts (Wayfinder): continuous Haiku-class checks -> bug-report lugs on failure
flow definitions: each flow step's expected_conditions -> a flow test
regression: a fixed bug adds a test that reproduces it (no fix without a guarding test)

test_quality_gate.principle: Test-at-birth guarantees a test EXISTS, not that it is CORRECT (blind-review HIGH: a wrong AC begets a wrong test, both gates pass green). The test must be REVIEWED before the lug opens, not after it closes.
rules: the test author is NOT the sole approver: a second party (QA/Jordy, or a fresh subagent for low-impact) reviews that the test actually exercises the AC's intent, before draft->open
mechanical tests are preferred precisely because they are auditable; an attested test must record WHAT it checked so the check itself can be reviewed
tests carry a reviewed_by + reviewed_at; an unreviewed test blocks promotion the same as a missing test

q2_coverage_maintenance.owner: spoke QA advisor (Jordy/qa-engineer) owns suite health; hub qa-guardian aggregates fleet-wide
q2_coverage_maintenance.registry: test-manifest.json (managed) maps every test -> owner(lug|flow|advisor) -> last_run -> result -> version. test_results table (storage layer) is the durable run history.
coverage_metrics: lug coverage: open work/impl lugs WITH a runnable verification_test / total (target: 100% before green)
flow coverage: priority flows WITH an e2e test / total
null_rate: checks with result=null (honest gaps) / total checks — DISCLOSED, not hidden
freshness: tests not run in >N versions/sessions flagged as stale (a coverage gap even if it once passed)
maintenance_rules: tests re-run on every minor version change of their owner (lug richness + test coverage are one concern)
stale tests surface in the QA advisor report + wakeup Quality Health section
a removed feature removes its tests (no derelict tests); a new feature adds tests at birth
certification_score = passes / (passes + fails + nulls) — coverage honesty is built into the score

q3_pre_user_quality_gate.principle: Agent tests FIRST. The user is only directed to work that is verified ready. Nothing is 'done' on the agent's say-so — the bolt must be tight (all checks=1) or gaps explicitly disclosed (null). 'Probably saved' is banned.

tiered_bar_by_visibility.count_aggregation_summary: TWO passes — mechanical (runs, keys present) AND semantic (inspect actual items in the count against known data). A count of 40 that's the wrong type is a semantic bug mechanical tests miss.
tiered_bar_by_visibility.user_facing_artifact: mechanical + semantic + EXPERIENTIAL — verified in the medium the user will use it (web: rendered in browser after load, not just well-formed on disk; CLI: actually run; doc: actually read)

q3_pre_user_quality_gate.enforcement: the Pattern Gate post-gate certifies the postcondition quality bar for the tier (not mere existence). Surfacing work to the user is itself a gated step; the gate blocks 'show user' until the tier's bar is met or gaps are disclosed with a null and a reason.

gate_integrity.principle: Read+Bash isolation makes the gate unable to AUTHOR success, but it can still pattern-match wrongly and miss a semantic error (blind-review HIGH). Two defenses:
rules: auditable gate: the gate emits the actual check it ran + what it observed (not just pass/fail), so the gate itself is reviewable on the event bus (a decision event)

fresh-actor rule at the visibility boundary: the gate certifying 'show user' for a user-facing artifact must NOT be the same model/agent that produced the work OR authored its test; for high-visibility output this tier is human or a distinct fresh model — closing the 'work, test, and gate all from one head' loophole

q3_pre_user_quality_gate.disclosure: when gaps remain, the user is shown the work WITH an explicit gap statement (what wasn't verified and why) — never silent partial completion presented as done.

q4_verification_logging.principle: No silent failures. Every test run and every failure is logged AND routed to the owning advisor.
q4_verification_logging.routing: a failure is routed to the owner advisor (QA for suite issues, the lug's owner for lug-test failures, Historian for trends). Escalation on repeated failure (retry cap) creates a Historian signal.
q4_verification_logging.reviewability: Historian mines test_results + gate-log for quality trends; QA advisor reports suite health; user reviews via the fleet dashboard Quality Health panel; wakeup brief surfaces a Quality Health section (coverage %, null rate, stale tests, failing suites).

gap_taxonomy.principle: 'Expose all the gaps' (north star) needs a DEFINITION of gap, or coverage is unmeasurable (review MED). A gap is a typed, detectable, surfaceable deficiency — each type has an owner-detector and a surface.

types.meaning: a check that ran null (not verified)
an acceptance criterion with no mapped test
code path/module with no exercising test
a mandated/recommended CG capability not present
a state change with no decision event / lug rationale
dead code, duplicate, file in wrong home
a deprecated path/marker still in use

gap_taxonomy.exposed_means: a gap is 'exposed' when it appears on its declared surface with a type + detector + (where possible) a remediation lug. The adoption gap report aggregates all types into the alignment roadmap. A dropped lug is 'gap-resolved' only if its drop is logged with a reason; otherwise it is gap-hidden (not allowed).

cross_cutting_map.lug_creation: test-at-birth gate (lug-reviewer) — AC<->test traceability before draft->open
cross_cutting_map.flow_execution: Pattern Gate pre/post — certifies preconditions + postcondition quality bar
cross_cutting_map.session_close: bolt certification — all checks tight or gaps disclosed; closeout test gate (v3 Step 0)
cross_cutting_map.advisor_runs: scout tests per advisor domain; certification = all advisor scouts green
cross_cutting_map.evolution: Trainer measures post-adoption test deltas vs pre-adoption baseline
cross_cutting_map.migration: v4_migrate no-orphan gate + additive checksum verification + bucket verification
cross_cutting_map.pre_user: tiered quality gate before any 'show user' step

constraints: No lug moves draft->open without a runnable verification_test covering every AC (hard gate)
Script-backed (mechanical) verification is preferred; attested/human only when no deterministic assertion is feasible
Counts/aggregations require BOTH mechanical and semantic passes
User-facing artifacts require experiential verification in the target medium
null is a first-class honest result (not a failure) but MUST be disclosed; certification_score counts nulls against coverage
No silent failures: every failure logged + routed to an owner
'Probably/should be' completion language is banned — state verified or state the gap
Removed features remove their tests; new features add tests at birth (no derelict, no untested)
tests: Test-at-birth gate: a lug with an AC lacking a test FAILS lug-reviewer draft->open; adding the test passes it
AC traceability: every AC in a sample lug maps to >=1 entry in its verify[]/test-manifest
Coverage compute: QA advisor reports lug coverage %, flow coverage %, null rate from test_results correctly
Stale detection: a test not run in >N versions is flagged stale in the QA report
Two-pass: a count feature passes mechanical (keys present) but a seeded semantic error (wrong item types in the count) is caught by the semantic pass and blocks 'done'
Experiential: a web artifact well-formed on disk but failing to render is BLOCKED from user-surfacing
Disclosure: work with a null check is surfaced to the user WITH an explicit gap statement, never as silent done
Logging: a test failure produces a test_results row + routes to the owner advisor; repeated failure escalates to Historian
Wakeup Quality Health section renders coverage %, null rate, stale count, failing suites

health.test_coverage: partial - q2 stale-test detection + gap_taxonomy (test-null/stale/failing) covered by test_qa_suite_health.py (6 tests). Uncovered: test-at-birth review gate (test_quality_gate second-party review), tiered pre-user quality gate (q3), experiential verification, failure routing (q4), QA advisor (Jordy) ownership.
open_questions: Coverage bar for green certification: 100% of open lugs test-covered (strict) vs a threshold with disclosed nulls (lenient)? — proposed: 100% test-PRESENT, null results allowed but disclosed
Test auto-generation autonomy: agent generates from ACs with QA verify (proposed) vs human-authored required for some tiers
QA advisor identity: spoke Jordy/qa-engineer (crew) vs a dedicated harness QA advisor — reconcile with crew migration

perceive: Read this spec + the v4 epic (AC10-13 lug quality, AC1-3 pattern gate). Read the storage layer test_results schema + test-manifest decision. Read CLAUDE.md anti-patterns (mechanical-only counts, skipping verification, 'probably saved'). Confirm QA advisor identity vs crew migration.
execute: 1. Make test-at-birth a hard gate in lug-reviewer (no draft->open without AC-covering runnable test). 2. Implement coverage computation + QA advisor suite-health report (test-manifest + test_results). 3. Implement the tiered pre-user quality gate as a Pattern Gate flow (internal/count/user-facing tiers) blocking 'show user'. 4. Add the wakeup Quality Health section. 5. Wire failure routing + no-silent-failure logging across track/bolt/gate-log/test_results. 6. Add stale-test detection.
verify: Run all tests[]. Confirm test-at-birth gate blocks an untested AC; two-pass catches a semantic count error; experiential gate blocks an unrendered web artifact; coverage + null rate compute correctly; failures log + route; Quality Health renders.
